name: Sarson da Saag
tagline: Punjabi mustard-greens stew — iron, vitamin K, and folate in one bowl
highlight: Long simmer + makki di roti — calcium oxalate breaks down, minerals stay

hero.alt: Bowl of dark-green mustard greens stew with a spoon of white butter melting on top
hero.caption: sarson da saag — mustard greens, slow-cooked

healthFacts: Mustard greens deliver 144µg vitamin K per cup — far above the daily target.
Combined with spinach and bathua, this dish hits 30% of daily iron and folate.
Slow simmer reduces oxalate load so the calcium and iron stay bioavailable.
Mustard's glucosinolates support liver phase-2 detox enzymes.

steps.title: Boil the greens
steps.detail: Wash all greens thoroughly. Pressure cook with 1.5 cups water and salt for 4 whistles. Let pressure release naturally. Cool slightly and blitz with a hand blender to a coarse purée.
steps.tip: A coarse purée — not silky-smooth — keeps the rustic Punjabi texture.

steps.title: Cook with cornmeal
steps.detail: Pour the purée back into a heavy pot. Stir in cornmeal slurry (2 tbsp cornmeal + ¼ cup water). Simmer gently on low for 25 minutes, stirring every 5 minutes — the saag should darken and thicken.
steps.tip: Cornmeal thickens AND soaks up the bitterness from raw mustard.

steps.title: Final tadka
steps.detail: Heat ghee in a small pan. Add chopped onion, ginger, garlic, and green chili. Cook till golden. Pour over the saag, stir half through, leave half on top. Serve with makki di roti and a knob of white butter.
steps.tip: Saag tastes better the next day — refrigerate, reheat with a splash of water.
